title: INA219 I²C Current and Power Monitor
design_question: How to measure pack current and cell bus voltage for Coulomb-counting SoC estimation over I²C?
selected_approach: INA219 breakout (Adafruit or similar) with 0.1 Ω / 1 % shunt resistor in series with battery negative rail. I²C address 0x40 (configurable via A0/A1 pads). 12-bit ADC; reads shunt voltage (±320 mV) and bus voltage (0–32 V); driver computes current = V_shunt / R_shunt. Polled at 10 Hz for Coulomb integration.
rationale: INA219 provides simultaneous current and voltage measurement over I²C, enabling both Coulomb counting and voltage-based SoC cross-checks from a single IC. 12-bit resolution at 0.1 Ω shunt = 0.8 mA current resolution — sufficient for 0.1 % SoC accuracy on a 1 Ah cell. Arduino: Adafruit_INA219 library.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Requires MCU ADC; offset drift ±1.5 % over temperature; resolution ≈ 30 mA — coarser than INA219.
Suitable for multi-cell packs but overkill for a single-pack BMS monitor; more complex calibration.

verification_notes: FACT: 'State-of-Charge Estimation via Coulomb Counting' recommends INA219 as the current measurement IC for Arduino-based BMS monitors.
supporting_fact_titles: CT Clamp Current Sensing and RMS Calculation
